steps.title: Create the bounce table
steps.content: The initial height is 22 feet, so the exponential model has point $(0,22)$. After one bounce, the maximum height is 85% of 22, which is 18.7 feet, so use $(1,18.7)$. Fit $y_1\sim a b^{x_1}$.

steps.title: Read the decay factor
steps.content: The regression gives $a=22$ and $b=0.85$, so $$h(n)=22(0.85)^n.$$ The base $0.85$ means each bounce reaches 85% of the previous height.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choice B has starting value 22 and decay factor 0.85. Choice D uses 85 as the starting value, and choices A and C use $0.22$ as the multiplier. Choice B is correct.
